description: Full-stack Gaussian splatting virtual tour SaaS platform with Next.js 14, Supabase, Stripe, and Three.js/gsplat.js

userStories.title: Monorepo scaffold with pnpm workspaces
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the monorepo scaffolded with pnpm workspaces, turbo.json, and the correct directory structure so all packages can be developed in parallel.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm-workspace.yaml exists with apps/* and packages/* globs
turbo.json configured with build, dev, typecheck, lint pipelines
apps/web is a Next.js 14 App Router project with TypeScript strict mode
packages/db, packages/ui, packages/types exist with package.json and tsconfig
Root tsconfig.json with strict: true and no-explicit-any ESLint rule
Tailwind CSS configured in apps/web with shadcn/ui initialized
pnpm dev from repo root boots the Next.js app successfully
Typecheck passes
CLAUDE.md at repo root with all 10 required sections per spec
userStories.notes: Use pnpm workspaces with turbo for build caching. Tailwind config must include the oklch color palette from the UI/UX spec: --bg: oklch(97.5% 0.006 68), --surface, --text-primary (space ink, never pure black), --brand (deep spatial indigo oklch(38% 0.16 268)), --accent (warm amber oklch(72% 0.165 62)). Configure Cormorant Garamond (variable, 300-700) for display and Geist (variable) for UI/body. Set up the fluid type scale with clamp() values. Add CSS custom properties for motion: --ease-out, --ease-in-out, --duration-fast/base/slow. Never use Inter or system-ui in marketing contexts. Zero any in tsconfig.

userStories.title: Shared TypeScript types package
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a shared types package with all core domain types so they can be imported across apps and packages.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/types exports interfaces for all 10 database tables: profiles, organizations, org_members, tours, scenes, waypoints, hotspots, floor_plans, billing_events, tour_views
Enums/unions for: plan ('free'|'pro'|'enterprise'), role ('owner'|'admin'|'editor'|'viewer'), tour status ('draft'|'published'|'archived'), category, content_type, splat_file_format
Billing plan limits type with tours, scenes_per_tour, storage_bytes, members per plan
No runtime code — types only
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This package must have zero runtime code — only TypeScript types and interfaces. Use branded types for UUIDs if desired. All jsonb fields (position_3d, default_camera_position, scene_positions) should have typed interfaces, e.g. Position3D = { x: number; y: number; z: number }.

userStories.title: Supabase schema migrations and RLS policies
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all 10 database tables created via migration SQL files with RLS policies so the database is secure by default.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/db contains migration SQL files for all 10 tables
All tables use UUID PKs with gen_random_uuid(), timestamptz defaults to now()
RLS enabled on every table

RLS policies enforce: profiles readable by owner, organizations readable by members, tours/scenes/waypoints/hotspots readable by org members (published tours publicly readable), org_members scoped to org, billing_events owner-only
Check constraints match spec: plan, role, status, category, content_type, splat_file_format
Unique constraints on: profiles.username, organizations.slug, tours.slug, billing_events.stripe_event_id
Foreign keys match spec exactly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: RLS is non-negotiable — no row accessible without valid JWT. Design RLS policies to use auth.uid() and join through org_members for org-scoped access. Published tours should be publicly readable (no JWT needed). The RBAC hierarchy is owner > admin > editor > viewer. Include the Supabase trigger for auto-creating a personal org on signup in this migration.

userStories.title: Typed Supabase client export
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a typed Supabase client package so all database queries are type-safe across the monorepo.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/db exports createBrowserClient() for client components using anon key
packages/db exports createServerClient() for server components/API routes using anon key with cookie handling
packages/db exports createServiceClient() for admin operations using service role key — clearly documented as server-only
Supabase types generated from schema and exported
Environment variable validation with Zod for all Supabase env vars
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY must NEVER be referenced in client-side code or NEXT_PUBLIC_ env vars. The service client export should include a comment warning. Use @supabase/ssr for cookie-based auth in Next.js App Router.

userStories.title: Auth pages — signup and login with OAuth
userStories.description: As a user, I want to sign up and log in with email/password or Google/Apple OAuth so I can access my account.
acceptanceCriteria: /signup page with email, password, display_name fields and consent checkbox (PDPA)
/login page with email/password and OAuth buttons
Google OAuth button and Apple OAuth button functional
Auth callback route handles OAuth redirect
On signup: Supabase trigger auto-creates a personal org with slug derived from display_name
Slug generation: trim → lowercase → non-alphanumeric to hyphens → transliterate non-ASCII → deduplicate with sequential -2, -3 (NOT random suffixes)
After login, redirect to /dashboard
Zod validation on all form inputs
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Auth pages should use the warm alabaster background (--bg), Geist font for form UI, and Cormorant Garamond for the page heading. Keep forms clean and minimal. Consent checkbox required for PDPA compliance. The slug deduplication MUST use sequential suffixes (-2, -3, etc.), never random. Implement as a Supabase database function/trigger.

userStories.title: Dashboard shell with sidebar layout and auth guard
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want a dashboard layout with sidebar navigation so I can navigate between dashboard sections.
acceptanceCriteria: /dashboard and all sub-routes protected by auth guard (redirect to /login if unauthenticated)
Sidebar: 200px fixed width, dark background (--text-primary), warm off-white text
Sidebar contains: logo, nav items (Tours, Scenes, Members, Settings), org switcher at bottom, plan badge (Free/Pro/Enterprise) pinned at bottom
Active nav item styled with left 3px brand-color bar + slightly lighter bg — no filled pill
Main content area: flex-1 with breadcrumb + page title
Mobile: bottom tab bar replaces sidebar (5 items max)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sidebar bg is --text-primary (dark, inverted surface for wayfinding contrast). Sidebar text is oklch(85% 0.015 68) warm off-white. Plan badge is always visible as a subtle upgrade nudge without being aggressive. Use shadcn/ui components where possible. The org switcher should show current org name and allow switching (multi-org support later).

userStories.title: Shared UI component library setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a shared UI component library with shadcn/ui components so the dashboard and public pages have consistent styling.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/ui exports reusable shadcn/ui components: Button, Input, Label, Card, Dialog, DropdownMenu, Tooltip, Badge, Skeleton, Toast
All components use the oklch color tokens from the design spec
Button variants include: default (brand), accent (amber CTA), outline, ghost, destructive
Loading skeleton components for cards and lists
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: shadcn/ui first, Tailwind utilities only, no raw CSS unless absolutely unavoidable. Amber accent buttons (--accent) for CTAs feel energetic against cool indigo brand. Never use glassmorphism, bounce animations on cards, or elastic on dropdowns.

userStories.title: Environment variable validation and config
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all environment variables validated at startup with Zod so misconfiguration fails fast.
acceptanceCriteria: Zod schema validates all 8 env vars from spec: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, RESEND_API_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL
Server-only vars (SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, RESEND_API_KEY) validated only on server
.env.local.example file with all variables and descriptions
App fails fast with clear error message if required vars are missing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Zod for validation. Ensure SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is never in a NEXT_PUBLIC_ variable or client bundle. The .env.local.example should match the CLAUDE.md documentation.

userStories.title: User profile API routes
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to view and update my profile so I can manage my account information.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/me returns current user profile (username, display_name, avatar_url, bio)
PATCH /api/me updates profile fields with Zod validation
Both routes require JWT authentication
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: All API inputs validated with Zod — reject before business logic. Use the anon Supabase client with user JWT, not the service role key.

userStories.title: Tour CRUD API routes with plan enforcement
userStories.description: As an org editor/admin/owner, I want to create, read, update, and delete tours so I can manage my organization's virtual tours.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/orgs/[orgId]/tours returns paginated tours for the org
POST /api/orgs/[orgId]/tours creates a tour with Zod-validated input
GET /api/orgs/[orgId]/tours/[tourId] returns tour detail with scenes
PATCH /api/orgs/[orgId]/tours/[tourId] updates tour fields
DELETE /api/orgs/[orgId]/tours/[tourId] deletes tour (owner/admin only)
POST /api/orgs/[orgId]/tours/[tourId]/publish changes status to published
Plan limit enforced on tour create: Free=2, Pro=unlimited, Enterprise=unlimited
Returns 403 with { error: 'PLAN_LIMIT_EXCEEDED', limit: 'tours', upgrade_url: '/pricing' } when limit hit
RBAC: editors can create/edit, admins can delete, viewers read-only, owner full access
Tour slug auto-generated with sequential deduplication (-2, -3)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: All inputs Zod-validated. Plan enforcement is server-side — do not skip it and rely on UI alone. The RBAC matrix must be checked on every operation. Slug deduplication uses sequential suffixes, never random.

userStories.title: Scene CRUD API routes with plan enforcement
userStories.description: As an org editor/admin/owner, I want to create, read, update, and delete scenes within a tour so I can build out tour content.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/orgs/[orgId]/tours/[tourId]/scenes returns scenes ordered by sort_order
POST /api/orgs/[orgId]/tours/[tourId]/scenes creates a scene with Zod validation
PATCH /api/orgs/[orgId]/tours/[tourId]/scenes/[sceneId] updates scene
DELETE /api/orgs/[orgId]/tours/[tourId]/scenes/[sceneId] deletes scene
Plan limit enforced on scene create: Free=3/tour, Pro=20/tour, Enterprise=unlimited
RBAC enforced per RBAC matrix
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Scene sort_order should support reordering. Plan enforcement checks scenes per tour, not total scenes.

userStories.title: Presigned upload flow for splat files with magic byte validation
userStories.description: As an org editor, I want to upload splat files directly to Supabase Storage via presigned URLs so uploads are fast and don't proxy through the API.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/orgs/[orgId]/tours/[tourId]/scenes/[sceneId]/upload returns a presigned URL
Presigned URL expires in 15 minutes
Client uploads directly to Supabase Storage (no API proxy)
After upload, server validates splat file format via magic bytes (NOT file extension)
Detected format stored in scenes.splat_file_format ('ply', 'splat', 'spz')
File stored at {org_id}/{tour_id}/{scene_id}/scene.{ext}
organizations.storage_used_bytes updated after upload
Plan limit enforced on storage: Free=1GB, Pro=50GB, Enterprise=500GB
Rate limit: 10 uploads/hour/org
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: NEVER trust file extensions — always validate via magic bytes server-side. PLY files start with 'ply\n', SPZ files have their own magic bytes, .splat files are raw gaussian data. No API proxying of uploads — presigned URLs only. Storage tracking must be atomic (use Supabase RPC or transaction).

userStories.title: Thumbnail upload for scenes
userStories.description: As an org editor, I want to manually upload thumbnails for scenes so tours have visual previews.
acceptanceCriteria: Thumbnail upload is a separate file input alongside splat upload
Stored at {org_id}/{tour_id}/{scene_id}/thumbnail.webp in the thumbnails bucket (public CDN)
Thumbnail URL saved to scenes.thumbnail_url
No auto-generation — manual upload only in v1
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The thumbnails bucket is public CDN. No thumbnail auto-generation in v1 — this is explicitly called out as a constraint. Accept common image formats and store as webp.

userStories.title: Supabase Storage bucket setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the three storage buckets configured with proper visibility and RLS so files are stored securely.
acceptanceCriteria: splat-files bucket: private, RLS mirrors tour visibility
thumbnails bucket: public CDN
assets bucket: private, org-scoped RLS
File path conventions enforced: splat at {org_id}/{tour_id}/{scene_id}/scene.{ext}, thumbnail at {org_id}/{tour_id}/{scene_id}/thumbnail.webp, assets at {org_id}/{type}/{filename}
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: RLS on storage buckets should use the same org membership checks as the database tables. Published tour splat files should be accessible publicly.

userStories.title: SplatViewer 3D component — core rendering
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view Gaussian splat scenes in an interactive 3D viewer so I can explore virtual tours.
acceptanceCriteria: Reusable <SplatViewer> component works in both public viewer and editor contexts
Renders .ply, .splat, .spz files via Three.js + gsplat.js
Mouse/trackpad orbit controls on desktop
Touch controls on mobile (pinch orbit, swipe scene)
Targets 30fps on mid-range devices
Progressive quality loading (blur(40px) → blur(0) as splat loads — NOT a spinner)
Error state: elegant fallback with thumbnail image + 'Preview unavailable'
Controls overlay: opacity 0 → 1 on hover, transition 200ms
Top-right: fullscreen and share buttons with backdrop-filter: blur(8px) tinted pill style
Fills container, no border-radius unless in card context
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the MOST important component — 'The SplatViewer is the product. Every other UI element is infrastructure.' The chrome disappears on hover. Controls ghost in from edges. Loading uses progressive blur-to-sharp reveal, NEVER a spinner. Show scene title fading in during load. The component MUST be shared between public and editor — do NOT duplicate it. Use backdrop-filter: blur(8px) with --surface tinted pill for controls.

userStories.title: SplatViewer — waypoints and hotspots overlay
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see and interact with waypoints and hotspots as 3D markers in the viewer so I can navigate between scenes and access content.
acceptanceCriteria: Waypoints rendered as hollow circles with directional arrow in --brand color
Hotspots rendered as filled circles with icon in --accent amber
Both are clickable 3D markers
Waypoint click navigates to target scene
Hotspot click shows info card (slides in from nearest edge)
Waypoints/hotspots scale on hover
Scene switcher UI at bottom-center: horizontal pill row, active scene has brand underline (only if >1 scene)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Waypoints: hollow circle + directional arrow, --brand color, scale on hover. Hotspots: filled circle with icon, --accent amber, info card slides in from nearest edge. These overlays must work in both public view and editor mode.

userStories.title: SplatViewer — WebXR support
userStories.description: As an Apple Vision Pro user, I want a 'View in Apple Vision Pro' button in the viewer so I can experience tours in spatial computing.
acceptanceCriteria: WebXR 'View in Apple Vision Pro' button at bottom-right of viewer
Button hidden when navigator.xr is unavailable (graceful degradation)
Button launches WebXR immersive session when available
Custom visionOS-style glyph icon (NOT generic VR goggles icon)
Button copy: 'View in Apple Vision Pro' — no emoji
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Only visible on Safari/visionOS UA or as always-visible on tour pages. Do NOT use the generic VR goggle icon — use a custom visionOS-inspired glyph. No emoji in button copy.

userStories.title: Tour editor — four-panel layout with scene list
userStories.description: As an org editor, I want a four-panel editor layout with a sortable scene list so I can manage tour scenes.
acceptanceCriteria: Four-panel layout: left scene list (240px), center 3D viewport, right inspector (280px), bottom floor plan drawer (collapsed by default)
Scene list is sortable via drag-and-drop with thumbnails
Scene reorder uses optimistic UI — update immediately, sync in background
[+] scene button at bottom of scene list
Header with inline-editable tour title, status badge, action buttons
Center viewport uses shared <SplatViewer> component
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Treat the editor like a creative tool, not a dashboard. The viewport fills all available space. Controls ghost on hover only. Panel resize uses transition: width var(--duration-base) var(--ease-out). Editor is desktop-only for editing — mobile shows view-only mode with a redirect to desktop notice.

userStories.title: Tour editor — click-to-place waypoints and hotspots
userStories.description: As an org editor, I want to place waypoints and hotspots in the 3D viewport by clicking so I can create interactive tour content.
acceptanceCriteria: Click-to-place mode for adding waypoints (cursor becomes crosshair + pulsing ring in 3D)
Click-to-place mode for adding hotspots
In edit mode: drag handle appears on hover, cursor changes to grab
Drag-to-reposition waypoints and hotspots in 3D space
Waypoint position updates use optimistic UI: CSS transform immediately, debounce API 500ms
Waypoint placement animation: scale(0) → scale(1) with spring cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1)
Viewport toolbar at bottom: camera reset, waypoint mode toggle, hotspot mode toggle, floor plan toggle
Waypoint/hotspot CRUD API: GET/POST for /scenes/[sceneId]/waypoints and /hotspots
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The spring cubic-bezier for waypoint placement is the ONLY acceptable spring animation. Never use bounce on cards or elastic on dropdowns. Optimistic UI is required — update UI immediately and sync in background.

userStories.title: Tour editor — properties inspector panel
userStories.description: As an org editor, I want a context-sensitive properties inspector so I can edit scene, waypoint, and hotspot properties.
acceptanceCriteria: Right panel (280px) shows context-sensitive properties for selected item (scene, waypoint, or hotspot)
Scene properties: title, description, camera defaults (advanced, collapsible), SEO (advanced, collapsible)
Waypoint properties: label, target scene (dropdown), icon, position
Hotspot properties: title, content_markdown, content_type, media_url, icon, position
All scene properties shown flat — do NOT use accordion for every section
Only collapse advanced settings (camera defaults, SEO)
Input labels above fields, not floating labels
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Do NOT use accordion for every section — show all scene properties flat. Only collapse advanced settings. Input labels above fields (easier to scan). No cards inside cards — flat inspector properties, no nesting.

userStories.title: Tour editor — floor plan panel
userStories.description: As an org editor, I want to upload a floor plan image and drag scene markers onto it so viewers can navigate spatially.
acceptanceCriteria: Floor plan drawer at bottom, collapsed by default, slides up with chevron toggle
Upload floor plan image to assets bucket
Drag scene markers onto floor plan to set positions
Scene positions stored as floor_plans.scene_positions jsonb [{scene_id, x, y}]
Minimap view shows floor plan with scene pins
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The floor plan drawer uses transition with --duration-base and --ease-out. Scene markers should match the scene list items for visual consistency.

userStories.title: Tour settings modal
userStories.description: As an org editor, I want a tour settings modal for metadata, SEO slug, and sharing options so I can configure how my tour appears publicly.
acceptanceCriteria: Tour settings accessible from editor header actions
Modal with: title, description, category, tags, location, cover image upload
SEO slug editing with preview of public URL
Sharing section: embed code snippet, QR code generation, direct link
Modal is acceptable UX for this complex form (per design spec)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Modals are acceptable for complex forms like tour settings. Use shadcn/ui Dialog component.

userStories.title: Dashboard tour grid with editorial layout
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to see my tours in an editorial masonry grid on the dashboard so I can manage my tour content.
acceptanceCriteria: Tour grid uses asymmetric masonry layout: mix 2-col + 1-col, first card is hero/featured size
NOT uniform identical cards — editorial presentation
Thumbnail hover shows mini SplatViewer preview (lazy-loaded on hover after 300ms debounce)
Empty state: not 'No tours yet' — show a demo tour card (greyed, non-editable) with 'Create your first tour' CTA explaining what tours are
Create tour button triggers tour creation flow
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The tour grid is NOT a uniform card grid — use asymmetric masonry with editorial presentation. The first card should be larger (hero/featured). On hover, load a mini SplatViewer preview lazily after 300ms debounce. Empty states should teach — explain what tours are, don't just say 'No tours yet'.

userStories.title: Landing page with live SplatViewer hero
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see an impressive landing page with a live 3D splat viewer hero so I understand the product's value immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section: live <SplatViewer> autoloading a featured tour, full-bleed 70vh min
Overlaid bottom-left editorial typeset: 'CLOUDTOUR / Spatial tours for the places worth remembering.' in Cormorant Garamond display size + 'Start for free →' CTA
Nav: logo (left), links (center), CTA (right)
Featured tours: horizontal scroll, large cards aspect-ratio 3/2, title overlay
How it works: editorial 3-column, large Cormorant step numbers (160px, 15% opacity) as background texture — NOT numbered step icons in cards
Pricing section: 3 columns, Pro visually elevated via scale transform (NOT a 'popular' badge)
FAQ section
Footer: minimal, left-aligned, link grid
Mobile: collapse hero to static splat thumbnail for performance, CTA remains prominent
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The live SplatViewer in the hero is THE differentiator — no competing platform ships this. Do NOT replace it with a video or static image on desktop. Use Cormorant Garamond at display size (clamp(3.5rem, 2.5rem + 5vw, 7rem)) for the hero heading. The how-it-works section uses large translucent Cormorant numerals as background texture, NOT card-based step icons. Pricing Pro column uses scale transform for elevation, NOT a 'popular' badge. Mobile collapses hero to static thumbnail for performance.

userStories.title: Explore page with filters and search
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to browse published tours with filters and search so I can discover interesting virtual tours.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/tours returns paginated, filterable tours (public endpoint)
/explore page with paginated grid of published tours
Filters: category (real_estate, tourism, museum, education, other), location, tags, sort (newest, popular, alphabetical)
Full-text search on tour title and description
Unauthenticated rate limit: 30 req/min
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a public page — no auth required. Use editorial card presentation consistent with the dashboard tour grid. Rate limiting via Vercel Edge Middleware.

userStories.title: Public tour page with SSR and SEO
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to view a published tour with full SEO metadata so tours are discoverable and shareable.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/tours/[slug] returns tour detail + scenes (public)
/tours/[slug] SSR page with metadata
Scene thumbnails grid alongside embedded <SplatViewer>
'Open in Apple Vision Pro' CTA
POST /api/tours/[slug]/view increments view_count
OG meta tags + Twitter Card meta
JSON-LD VirtualLocation schema markup
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: SSR for SEO — use Next.js generateMetadata. The SplatViewer on this page is the same shared component. View counter should use viewer_ip_hash for basic deduplication.

userStories.title: SEO infrastructure — sitemap, robots.txt, meta
userStories.description: As a search engine, I want a dynamic sitemap and robots.txt so all published content is discoverable.
acceptanceCriteria: Dynamic sitemap.xml covering all published tours + blog + static pages
robots.txt blocks /dashboard/* and /api/*
All public pages have OG + Twitter Card meta tags
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Next.js metadata API and sitemap generation. Ensure /dashboard and /api paths are blocked in robots.txt.

userStories.title: Pricing page
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see a pricing comparison page so I can choose the right plan.
acceptanceCriteria: /pricing page with 3-column plan comparison table
Free ($0): 2 tours, 3 scenes/tour, 1GB storage, 1 member
Pro ($29/mo): unlimited tours, 20 scenes/tour, 50GB storage, 10 members
Enterprise ($199/mo): unlimited everything, 500GB storage, unlimited members
Pro column visually elevated via scale transform (NOT a 'popular' badge)
CTA buttons link to signup/checkout
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Pro column elevated with scale transform only — no 'popular' badge. Use amber accent CTA buttons. Keep consistent with the pricing section on the landing page.

userStories.title: Org members management and invite flow
userStories.description: As an org owner/admin, I want to invite members and manage roles so I can collaborate with my team.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/orgs/[orgId]/members returns org members with roles
POST /api/orgs/[orgId]/members sends invitation (admin/owner only per RBAC)
DELETE /api/orgs/[orgId]/members removes a member
Plan limit enforced on invite: Free=1, Pro=10, Enterprise=unlimited
Member invite uses slide-over panel (NOT a modal — per design spec)
/invite/[token] page accepts org invitations
Invited user's org_members row: user_id null until accepted, invited_email set
On accept: user_id populated, joined_at set
RBAC: owner > admin > editor > viewer hierarchy enforced
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Member invite MUST use a slide-over panel, NOT a modal (per design spec — modals are not acceptable for member invite). Plan enforcement on invite count is server-side. Send invite email via Resend.

userStories.title: Stripe billing integration — checkout and webhooks
userStories.description: As an org owner, I want to subscribe to a paid plan via Stripe so my organization can access premium features.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/orgs/[orgId]/billing creates Stripe Checkout session
Stripe Checkout redirects user to Stripe-hosted payment page
POST /api/webhooks/stripe handles customer.subscription.* and invoice.* events
Webhook handler verifies stripe-signature header before processing
billing_events table records all webhook events with idempotency on stripe_event_id
Organization plan updated on successful subscription
Org billing portal accessible from /dashboard/settings via Stripe Billing Portal
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Stripe webhook handler MUST verify stripe-signature header — never process unverified webhooks. Idempotency via unique constraint on billing_events.stripe_event_id. Use STRIPE_SECRET_KEY and STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET server-side only.

userStories.title: Rate limiting via Vercel Edge Middleware
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want rate limiting on all API endpoints so the system is protected from abuse.
acceptanceCriteria: Vercel Edge Middleware enforces rate limits
Authenticated endpoints: 100 req/min
Unauthenticated endpoints: 30 req/min
Upload endpoints: 10 uploads/hour/org
Rate limit exceeded returns 429 with clear error message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implement in Next.js middleware.ts. Consider using Vercel KV or an in-memory store for rate limit counters.

userStories.title: Blog with MDX and RSS feed
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to read blog posts and subscribe via RSS so I can stay updated on CloudTour news.
acceptanceCriteria: /blog page listing posts with categories
MDX content for blog posts
RSS feed at /blog/rss.xml or /feed.xml
Blog posts included in dynamic sitemap
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use MDX with Next.js App Router. Blog posts should use Cormorant Garamond for titles and Geist for body text.

userStories.title: Static pages — about, contact, privacy, terms
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to access about, contact, privacy policy, and terms of service pages so I can learn about and trust the platform.
acceptanceCriteria: /about page with company information
/contact page with contact form
/privacy page with PDPA-compliant privacy policy
/terms page with terms of service
All pages included in sitemap
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Privacy policy must be PDPA compliant — mention data collection, consent, and account deletion within 30 days. Contact form should use Zod validation.

userStories.title: Resend email templates
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want transactional email templates via Resend so users receive important notifications.
acceptanceCriteria: Welcome email sent on signup
Tour published notification email
Member invite email with /invite/[token] link
Password reset email
All emails sent via Resend API
RESEND_API_KEY used server-side only
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Resend for all transactional email. Keep templates clean and consistent with the brand — warm alabaster background, brand indigo accents.

userStories.title: Onboarding wizard for first-time users
userStories.description: As a new user, I want an onboarding wizard on first login so I understand how to get started with CloudTour.
acceptanceCriteria: Onboarding wizard triggers on first login (check profile or flag)
3 steps max per screen, progress dots (not numbered steps)
Guides user through: profile setup, first tour creation, upload first scene
Can be skipped/dismissed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Progressive disclosure — 3 steps max per screen. Use progress dots, not numbered steps. Keep it brief and helpful.

userStories.title: Empty states, loading skeletons, and error boundaries
userStories.description: As a user, I want polished empty states, loading skeletons, and error boundaries so the app feels complete and handles edge cases gracefully.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty tour list: demo tour card (greyed) with 'Create your first tour' CTA
Empty scene list: inline drop zone with format support text (.ply .splat .spz)
Loading skeletons for all list/grid views (dashboard content staggered fade-up, 40px transform, 30ms delay between items)
Error boundaries on all major page sections with friendly fallback UI
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Empty states that teach — not just 'Nothing here'. Dashboard page entrance uses staggered fade-up animation: 40px transform, 30ms delay between items. Loading skeletons should match the layout shape of actual content.
